title: Traditional Platforms vs Unchained Commerce for Payment Solutions That Actually Work for Nigerian Freelancers: What Actually Works
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Creators in Nigeria, Pakistan, Ghana, Bangladesh, and dozens of other countries cannot use the platforms everyone else takes for granted. This changes that.

content.text: As a digital creator in Nigeria, you've put your heart and soul into crafting a unique digital product, only to be met with the frustrating reality of being blocked from collecting payment due to geographic restrictions. You're not alone in this struggle, as many freelancers in Africa and other developing countries face similar challenges when trying to sell their digital products globally. The conventional payment platforms like PayPal, Stripe, and Payhip often fall short, leaving you wondering how to overcome these obstacles and get paid for your work.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When evaluating payment solutions, you're essentially comparing the limitations of traditional platforms against the freedom and flexibility offered by crypto-native infrastructure. The traditional platforms, such as PayPal and Stripe, are often restrictive, requiring Know Your Customer (KYC) verification and sometimes even freezing accounts without notice. On the other hand, crypto-native infrastructure, like that offered by Unchained Commerce, provides a more open and accessible alternative, allowing you to receive payments from anywhere in the world without the need for lengthy verification processes.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional payment platforms like Gumroad and Payhip often break down for digital creators in restricted countries due to their strict KYC requirements and limited geographic support. These platforms may not be available in your country, or they may require you to jump through hoops to verify your identity, only to reject your application in the end. This can be devastating, especially when you've already invested time and effort into creating your digital product and are eager to start selling it.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, does things differently. It allows you to receive payments in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for KYC verification or traditional banking infrastructure. This means that you can sell your digital products to anyone, anywhere in the world, without worrying about geographic restrictions or payment processing limitations. With crypto-native infrastructure, you have more control over your finances and can focus on what matters most - creating and selling your digital products.
The reality is that traditional payment platforms are not designed to support digital creators in restricted countries, and it's time to look for alternative solutions that actually work.
The Real Cost Difference

One of the most significant advantages of crypto-native infrastructure is the cost difference. Traditional payment platforms often charge hefty fees for transactions, which can eat into your profits and leave you with less money in your pocket. In contrast, crypto-native infrastructure typically charges lower fees, or sometimes no fees at all, depending on the specific solution you choose. This can make a huge difference in your bottom line, especially if you're selling digital products at a low price point.
Which One to Use and Why

So, which one should you use? If you're a digital creator in a restricted country, the answer is clear: crypto-native infrastructure is the way to go. With its greater flexibility, lower fees, and lack of KYC requirements, it's the perfect solution for those who want to sell their digital products globally without restrictions. Unchained Commerce offers a range of products, including the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, that can help you get started with crypto-native infrastructure and start selling your digital products to a global audience.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

If you're a digital creator in Nigeria or another restricted country, you can sell your digital products globally without geographic restrictions by using crypto-native infrastructure. This allows you to receive payments in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for KYC verification or traditional banking infrastructure. You can also sell ebooks from Africa using crypto, and receive international payments for digital products from anywhere in the world. Whether you're in Pakistan, Bangladesh, or another country with limited access to traditional payment platforms, crypto-native infrastructure can help you overcome these obstacles and get paid for your work.
Crypto-native infrastructure is the key to unlocking true platform independence, allowing you to sell your digital products to anyone, anywhere in the world, without restrictions.
Related Topics Worth Reading

For digital creators in developing countries, selling digital products online can be a game-changer. However, it's not just about selling ebooks or courses - it's about having the freedom to create and sell any type of digital product you want, without restrictions. Global digital product sales, crypto payments, and international transactions are all connected, and crypto-native infrastructure is the key to making it all work seamlessly.
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching your digital product store, make sure you have the right infrastructure in place. This includes setting up a Core Shop Engine to handle automated delivery and payment verification, integrating Web3 Wallet Connect to enable multi-chain QR code payments, and configuring your Admin Dashboard to track transactions and orders. You should also consider the type of digital products you want to sell, your target audience, and the pricing strategy you'll use. By taking the time to set up your store correctly, you can ensure a smooth and successful launch, and start selling your digital products to a global audience.

Success as a digital creator means being able to focus on what matters most - creating and selling your digital products - without worrying about payment processing limitations or geographic restrictions.
